meta.note: Indian population-calibrated reference ranges. Values reflect ICMR / RGUHS BNYS clinical guidelines. Ranges may vary slightly by laboratory method — always correlate clinically.
meta.south_asian_note: South Asian populations carry higher cardiometabolic risk at lower BMI and HbA1c thresholds. Interpret accordingly.

Hemoglobin.notes: ICMR-adjusted lower threshold for Indian population. Anaemia highly prevalent — Hb < 11 g/dL warrants iron studies.
Hemoglobin.bnys_insight: Iron-deficiency anaemia: increase dietary iron (amaranth, drumstick, jaggery), vitamin C co-ingestion, avoid tea/coffee with meals. Fasting therapy contraindicated below 10 g/dL.

WBC (Total Leucocyte Count).notes: Leucocytosis > 11000 suggests infection/inflammation. Leucopenia < 4000 in viral illness or bone marrow suppression.
WBC (Total Leucocyte Count).bnys_insight: Chronic low-grade leucocytosis may indicate subclinical inflammation — address gut health, sleep, and stress. Tulsi, Guduchi support immune regulation.

Neutrophils.notes: Neutrophilia > 70%: bacterial infection. Neutropenia < 40%: viral, drugs, bone marrow issue.

Lymphocytes.notes: Lymphocytosis > 40%: viral infection, CLL. Lymphopenia < 20%: HIV, TB, corticosteroid use.

Eosinophils.notes: Eosinophilia > 6% common in India — allergy, parasitic infection, ABPA.
Eosinophils.bnys_insight: Persistent eosinophilia with GI symptoms — stool examination for parasites mandatory. Neem, turmeric support anti-parasitic action.

Platelets.notes: Thrombocytopenia < 100,000 in dengue, ITP, SLE. Thrombocytosis > 400,000 in reactive or CML.
Platelets.bnys_insight: Dengue thrombocytopenia — papaya leaf extract (Carica papaya) has evidence (RCT) for platelet improvement. Avoid invasive procedures below 50,000.

MCV.notes: Microcytic (< 80): iron deficiency, thalassaemia. Macrocytic (> 100): B12/folate deficiency, hypothyroidism.
confirm_tests: Haemoglobin electrophoresis if microcytic with normal ferritin

Hematocrit (PCV).notes: Reflects red cell mass. Elevated in polycythaemia vera or dehydration.

Vitamin D (25-OH).india_note: Paradoxically, > 80% of Indians are deficient despite high sun exposure — melanin, sunscreen, indoor lifestyle, vegetarian diet reduce synthesis. Target 40–60 ng/mL for optimal BNYS outcomes.
Vitamin D (25-OH).bnys_insight: Sunlight exposure (10:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 20 minutes, 40% skin exposure) is first-line. Supplement: Cholecalciferol 60,000 IU weekly × 8 weeks, then 2,000 IU daily maintenance. Monitor at 3 months.

Vitamin B12.india_note: Vegetarians have 4× higher risk of B12 deficiency. Treat grey zone (200–300) symptomatically in vegetarians.
Vitamin B12.bnys_insight: Dairy, eggs (if lacto-ovo), fortified foods. Supplement: Methylcobalamin 1500 µg daily or 500 µg TDS. IM cyanocobalamin 1000 µg × 10 days for severe neurological deficiency.
confirm_tests: Intrinsic factor antibody if pernicious anaemia suspected

Fasting Blood Glucose.india_note: South Asians develop T2DM at lower BMI (≥ 23 kg/m²). Prediabetes management is critical — lifestyle intervention can prevent progression in 58% of cases (DPP trial).
Fasting Blood Glucose.bnys_insight: Naturopathic protocol: Karela (bitter gourd) 50 mL juice fasting, Gurmar (Gymnema sylvestre) 400 mg BD, Fenugreek seed soaking overnight then consuming. Structured walking 45 min/day reduces FBG significantly.

HbA1c.south_asian_note: South Asian T2DM risk begins at HbA1c > 5.7%. Earlier intervention indicated compared to Western populations. Target HbA1c < 7.0% for managed T2DM.
HbA1c.bnys_insight: HbA1c reflects 3-month average. 1% reduction possible with: combined yoga + diet + fasting therapy programme over 12 weeks. Amla (Indian gooseberry) has RCT evidence for HbA1c reduction.

Fasting Insulin.notes: HOMA-IR > 2.5 indicates insulin resistance. Optimal fasting insulin < 8 µIU/mL for metabolic health.
Fasting Insulin.india_note: Indians have inherently higher insulin resistance — even lean individuals may have HOMA-IR > 2.5 due to central adiposity.
Fasting Insulin.bnys_insight: Intermittent fasting, low-glycaemic diet, aerobic exercise reduce fasting insulin significantly. Berberine 500 mg TDS has RCT evidence comparable to metformin for insulin resistance.
confirm_tests: PCOS workup if female with IR

Iron (Serum).notes: Must interpret with TIBC and ferritin for complete iron status.

Ferritin.notes: Ferritin < 30 ng/mL even with normal Hb indicates depleted iron stores. Ferritin is also an acute-phase reactant — elevated in infection/inflammation masking true deficiency.
Ferritin.bnys_insight: Iron-rich foods: dried figs, raisins, curry leaves, drumstick leaves, moringa, til (sesame). Combine with vitamin C sources. Avoid tea/coffee within 1 hour of iron-rich meals.
confirm_tests: CRP (to exclude inflammatory state elevating ferritin)

ALT (SGPT).notes: Most liver-specific transaminase. Elevated in hepatitis, NAFLD, drugs, alcohol. > 3× ULN is significant.
ALT (SGPT).bnys_insight: Hepato-protective BNYS herbs: Bhumi amla (Phyllanthus niruri) 300 mg TDS, Milk thistle (silymarin 140 mg TDS), Kutki (Picrorhiza kurroa) 200 mg BD. Avoid hepatotoxic supplements.

AST (SGOT).notes: Less liver-specific than ALT — also elevated in muscle, heart. AST:ALT ratio > 2:1 suggests alcoholic liver disease.

Total Bilirubin.notes: Direct > 0.3: conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia (obstructive). Indirect > 1.0: haemolysis or Gilbert's syndrome.
Total Bilirubin.bnys_insight: Mild jaundice with normal liver enzymes: screen for Gilbert's syndrome (benign). Fasting aggravates Gilbert's — avoid prolonged fasts.

Albumin.notes: Marker of hepatic synthetic function and nutritional status. Albumin < 3.5 in cirrhosis, malnutrition, nephrotic syndrome.

GGT.notes: Sensitive marker of alcohol consumption, enzyme induction, biliary disease. Elevated in NAFLD before ALT rises.

Alkaline Phosphatase.notes: Elevated in cholestasis, bone disease, pregnancy. Physiologically elevated in children and adolescents — cannot use adult range.
confirm_tests: Bone density scan if bone source suspected

Serum Creatinine.india_note: Indian population has lower muscle mass — creatinine may be lower at equivalent GFR. Use CKD-EPI equation. Vegetarians have inherently lower creatinine.
Serum Creatinine.bnys_insight: CKD naturopathic support: low-protein plant-based diet, Punarnava (Boerhavia diffusa) 500 mg BD, avoid nephrotoxic herbs (aristolochic acid, comfrey). Monitor closely.

eGFR.notes: Calculate using CKD-EPI formula for most accurate estimation in Indian patients.

BUN (Blood Urea Nitrogen).notes: BUN:Creatinine ratio > 20:1 suggests pre-renal cause (dehydration, GI bleed). < 10:1 suggests intrinsic renal disease or low protein intake.

Uric Acid.notes: Hyperuricaemia > 7.0 associated with gout, CKD, metabolic syndrome.
Uric Acid.india_note: Rising hyperuricaemia in India linked to high fructose corn syrup, meat consumption, and diuretic use in hypertension.
Uric Acid.bnys_insight: Low-purine diet (avoid red meat, organ meat, shellfish, alcohol). Cherry extract 480 mg/day has RCT evidence for reducing gout flares. Dandelion root supports uric acid excretion.
confirm_tests: Joint fluid crystal analysis if gout

Urine Microalbumin-Creatinine Ratio (ACR.notes: First sign of diabetic nephropathy and hypertensive nephropathy. Screen annually in all diabetics and hypertensives.
confirm_tests: Repeat ACR on 2 more occasions over 3 months

TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone).india_note: Iodine deficiency thyroid disease prevalent in inland states. Hypothyroidism affects 1 in 10 Indians, higher in women. TSH > 2.5 in pregnancy needs treatment.
TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone).bnys_insight: Hypothyroidism BNYS support: seaweed (Kelp — use cautiously, iodine source), Ashwagandha 300 mg BD (RCT evidence for TSH normalisation in subclinical hypo), yoga (Sarvangasana, Matsyasana) for thyroid stimulation. Soy, cruciferous vegetables reduce absorption — separate by 4 hours from levothyroxine.

Free T4.notes: Low in hypothyroidism. Elevated in hyperthyroidism or acute illness. Test alongside TSH for complete picture.

Free T3.notes: Active thyroid hormone. Useful in monitoring T3 toxicosis and conversion efficiency assessment.
confirm_tests: Reverse T3 if suspected impaired conversion

TPO Antibody.notes: Positive in Hashimoto's thyroiditis and Graves' disease. Strong predictor of progression to overt hypothyroidism.
TPO Antibody.bnys_insight: Autoimmune thyroiditis — gluten-free trial for 3 months may reduce TPO antibody levels (evidence: PMID 27238603). Selenium 200 µg/day (Brazil nuts or supplement) reduces TPO antibodies (multiple RCTs).

Total Cholesterol.notes: Total cholesterol alone has limited clinical utility — always interpret with LDL, HDL, non-HDL, and TG.
confirm_tests: Full lipid panel with LDL, HDL, TG, non-HDL

LDL Cholesterol.india_note: South Asian guidelines recommend more aggressive LDL targets: < 70 mg/dL for very high CV risk, < 55 mg/dL post-MI.
LDL Cholesterol.bnys_insight: Plant stanols/sterols 2 g/day reduce LDL by 10–15%. Red yeast rice (10 mg monacolin K) equivalent to low-dose statin (RCT evidence). Flaxseed 30 g/day, psyllium 10 g/day, oat beta-glucan 3 g/day all have evidence.
confirm_tests: Coronary calcium score if intermediate risk

HDL Cholesterol.india_note: Indians have characteristically low HDL even with normal-weight BMI — a major CV risk factor in this population.
HDL Cholesterol.bnys_insight: Exercise (aerobic × 30 min daily) raises HDL 3–9%. Niacin (nicotinic acid) 1–2 g/day raises HDL significantly but flush side-effect limits use. Moderate wine → replaced by grape seed extract in BNYS practice.

Triglycerides.india_note: Hypertriglyceridaemia disproportionately prevalent in India — refined carbohydrate diet, alcohol, uncontrolled diabetes.
Triglycerides.bnys_insight: Most modifiable lipid fraction — carbohydrate restriction and omega-3 (EPA+DHA 3–4 g/day) reduce TG 20–50%. Fish oil RCTs strongest evidence. Alcohol cessation essential.

HbA1c.south_asian_note: South Asian populations carry T2DM risk at HbA1c > 5.7% — earlier than WHO/ADA thresholds designed for Western populations. Indian populations develop T2DM at younger age, lower BMI, and higher CV risk. Aggressive lifestyle intervention is indicated in prediabetes range.
HbA1c.bnys_insight: 12-week structured naturopathic programme: caloric restriction + 45-min brisk walk daily + yoga (Surya namaskar × 12 rounds) + Karela 50 mL juice fasting + Fenugreek 10 g soaked seeds can reduce HbA1c by 0.5–1.0% in prediabetes.

hs-CRP (High Sensitivity CRP).notes: Values > 10 mg/L suggest acute infection or tissue injury rather than chronic inflammation — repeat when acute event resolved.
hs-CRP (High Sensitivity CRP).bnys_insight: Anti-inflammatory BNYS protocol: curcumin (BCM-95 formulation) 500 mg TDS has RCT-level evidence for CRP reduction. Omega-3 FA 3 g/day, elimination of refined carbohydrates, sleep optimisation (7–9 hrs). Mediterranean diet reduces hs-CRP significantly.
confirm_tests: ANA, ANCA, RF if autoimmune suspected

ESR (Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate).age_correction: Upper limit ≈ Age/2 (male) or Age/2+5 (female) — Westergren method
ESR (Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate).notes: Non-specific marker of inflammation. Elevated in anaemia, pregnancy, infection, autoimmune disease, malignancy.

Homocysteine.india_note: Hyperhomocysteinaemia prevalent in vegetarians due to B12 deficiency. B12 + B6 + folate supplementation reduces homocysteine.
Homocysteine.bnys_insight: Treat underlying B12/folate deficiency first. Riboflavin 10 mg/day if MTHFR polymorphism (use methylfolate 400 µg/day). Betaine 3 g/day as adjunct.

Ferritin (as inflammatory marker).notes: Ferritin > 500 — exclude adult-onset Still's disease, haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, hepatic disease, haemochromatosis. In metabolic syndrome, ferritin elevates without iron overload.

LDH (Lactate Dehydrogenase).notes: Non-specific marker — elevated in haemolysis, MI, PE, hepatitis, lymphoma. Useful in dengue, COVID complications.
confirm_tests: Context-specific: CK-MB/troponin for cardiac, haptoglobin for haemolysis, CT-PA for PE

ANA (Anti-Nuclear Antibody).notes: Positive ANA alone is insufficient for SLE diagnosis — up to 15% of healthy population may have low-titre positive. Requires specific ENA panel for diagnosis.

Sodium.notes: Hyponatraemia: excess water, SIADH, diuretics, hypothyroidism. Hypernatraemia: dehydration, diabetes insipidus.

Potassium.notes: Hypokalaemia: diarrhoea, diuretics, hyperaldosteronism. Hyperkalaemia: CKD, ACE inhibitors, Addison's disease.
Potassium.bnys_insight: Dietary potassium: bananas, coconut water, sweet potato, spinach. For hypokalaemia — avoid loop diuretics without monitoring. Hypertension patients on naturo protocol often achieve BP control allowing diuretic reduction.
confirm_tests: ECG (both hypo and hyperkalaemia cause arrhythmia)

Calcium (Total).notes: Always correct for albumin: corrected Ca = total Ca + 0.8 × (4 – albumin). Ionised calcium is more reliable.
